title: 4.3 Renowned Ethiopian Geneticist
description: Dr. Tewolde Berhan Gebre Egziabher and Dr. Melaku Worede: their contributions to genetics and seed conservation.

questions.question: Who is the renowned Ethiopian geneticist and agronomist discussed in this unit?
Dr. Melaku Worede is renowned for employing science to benefit which group of people?
What was Dr. Melaku Worede's work focused on saving from extinction?
What award did Dr. Melaku Worede receive in 1989?
Dr. Melaku Worede was awarded in 1989 for preserving what?
options: Ethiopia's genetic wealth by building one of the finest seed conservation centres in the world
questions.correctAnswer: Ethiopia's genetic wealth by building one of the finest seed conservation centres in the world

questions.question: Dr. Melaku Worede played a key role in the establishment of what centre?
options: The Plant Genetic Resource Centre in Addis Ababa
questions.correctAnswer: The Plant Genetic Resource Centre in Addis Ababa

questions.question: In what year did Dr. Melaku Worede become the Director of the Plant Genetic Resource Centre?
For how many years did Dr. Melaku Worede serve as Director of the Plant Genetic Resource Centre until retirement?
After retirement, Dr. Melaku Worede developed pioneer work on what type of conservation?
options: Farming based native seed (landrace) conservation, enhancement and utilization
questions.correctAnswer: Farming based native seed (landrace) conservation, enhancement and utilization

questions.question: What type of seeds did Dr. Melaku Worede develop and grow, such as durum wheat?
Dr. Melaku Worede grew native seeds like durum wheat without using what?
By what percentage did Dr. Melaku Worede's native seeds exceed their high input counterparts in yield?
By what percentage did Dr. Melaku Worede's native seeds exceed the original farmers' cultivars in yield?
What is interphase defined as in the unit summary?
options: The stage of cell division where chromosomes separate
The stage of preparation where a cell exhibits growth and DNA synthesis
The stage where the nuclear membrane breaks down
questions.correctAnswer: The stage of preparation where a cell exhibits growth and DNA synthesis

questions.question: Cell division is a basic process in all living things where a parent cell divides into cells called what?
In multicellular organisms, cell division is a means of growth and what else?
In unicellular organisms, cell division is equivalent to what?
Mitosis is division of what type of cells to form identical daughter cells?
Mitosis has how many stages?
What are the four stages of mitosis?
options: Prophase I, metaphase I, anaphase I, and telophase I

questions.question: Meiosis is a division of reproductive cells resulting in daughter cells with what number of chromosomes compared to the mother cell?
The daughter cells produced by meiosis are specifically called what?
In meiosis, a cell divides how many times?
Meiosis results in how many daughter cells?
Which division of meiosis involves homologous chromosomes separating and moving to opposite cells?
Which division of meiosis involves sister chromatids separating and moving to opposite poles?
What is the best analogy given in the textbook for homologous chromosomes?
options: A pair of shoes with the same number
questions.correctAnswer: A pair of shoes with the same number

questions.question: What is the haploid abbreviation used in the textbook?
What is the diploid abbreviation used in the textbook?
If a cell has $2n$ = 40 and divides by meiosis, how many cells are formed at the end of meiosis I?
If a cell has $2n$ = 40 and divides by meiosis, how many cells are formed at the end of meiosis II?
